# Source Card: Consensus Statement of the Academy of Nutrition and Dietetics/A.S.P.E.N. — Indicators Recommended for the Identification and Documentation of Pediatric Malnutrition (Undernutrition)

## Summary
The 2014 AND/A.S.P.E.N. consensus statement (Becker et al.; endorsed by the American Academy of Pediatrics) recommends a single standardized set of anthropometric indicators to identify and document pediatric undernutrition in children ages 1 month to 18 years across acute, ambulatory, and residential settings. For a single data point the indicators are z-scores (weight-for-height/length, BMI-for-age, length/height-for-age, or MUAC); with two or more data points they add weight-gain velocity (age <2y), weight loss (age 2-20y), deceleration in weight-for-length/height z-score, and inadequate nutrient intake. For the pediatric CDS module this establishes the indicator vocabulary, age scope, and the diagnosis-vs-screening boundary; it is a proposal for the CDS converter, not a diagnosis/treatment directive.

## Key evidence (with exact-passage locators)
- (ev_001) Single-datapoint indicators = z-scores for weight-for-height/length, BMI-for-age, length/height-for-age, or MUAC — locator: Abstract — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:04a895d3e276555f5e69cb0573f72d18b454582e90dc37148aeffa477c1ad0cc]"
- (ev_002) Multi-datapoint indicators + age applicability (velocity <2y, weight loss 2-20y) — locator: Abstract — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:d66f1edb2b64aeb65a55dfe90b85fe4f5d85f2f7e98f0d9465e154d8b39a696f]"
- (ev_003) Applies to ages 1 month to 18 years — locator: Abstract — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:c855ce82a4d47e26606731b80a382d0e2a61173883d36ed5c2144e299c5c8b08]"
- (ev_004) Multi-setting scope (acute, ambulatory/outpatient, residential) — locator: Abstract — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:d1f5b4b94f564e32c8fe274e81626c561efdbb69bfef866fd9c90c5a01e5843e]"
- (ev_005) Screening criteria are out of scope (diagnosis/documentation only) — locator: Abstract — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:c065a72d94fecf15b26d001bd36a6b9c6fcb47a10c11fc46a56a6b3e87db592e]"
- (ev_006) Use as many data points as available — locator: Abstract — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:556021e326b6c4216906fb3ae002897f758f31ed74aa4c126c98c57fe803e9cc]"

## Population / assay / lifecycle notes
- Population: pediatric, ages 1 month to 18 years; some multi-datapoint indicators are age-banded (weight-gain velocity for children <2y; weight loss for 2-20y). z-scores are computed on sex-specific growth references.
- Assay/method: none are laboratory-analyzer/assay-dependent — they are anthropometric (weight, length/height, BMI, MUAC) and dietary-intake based. Critically, the z-score indicators are GROWTH-REFERENCE-STANDARD dependent (WHO vs CDC growth charts); the specific reference-per-age mapping (e.g., WHO <2y, CDC 2-20y) is stated in the full-text body, which was not retrievable verbatim, so it is not asserted on this card.
- Lifecycle: consensus statement effective 2014-11 (NCP 2015;30(1):147-161); twin-published in J Acad Nutr Diet 2014;114(12):1988-2000 (PMID 25458748, DOI 10.1016/j.jand.2014.08.026). No retirement/superseding version recorded.

## Scope-exit / referral relevance (if any)
- This statement explicitly scopes itself to identification/documentation of undernutrition, NOT nutritional-risk screening (ev_005) — a hard boundary for the CDS: these indicators must not be repurposed as screening triggers. It also does not define treatment/dosing/referral actions; downstream intervention pathways are out of scope of the extracted passages.

## Limitations & conflicts
- GAP — severity-cutoff z-score bands NOT locatable verbatim in source (25422273) as accessed 2026-07-18: the mild/moderate/severe z-score ranges for weight-for-length/height, BMI-for-age, length/height-for-age, and MUAC (single data point) live in the full-text severity table, which is paywalled (WebFetch 402/403 on NCP, JAND, ScienceDirect, ResearchGate; firecrawl out of credits). Numeric bands are therefore NOT asserted; they must be sourced from the full-text table before any CDS cutoff is encoded.
- GAP — multi-datapoint percentage/decline cutoffs NOT locatable verbatim in source: weight-gain-velocity (% of norm), weight-loss (% usual body weight), z-score-deceleration (magnitude of z-score decline), and inadequate-nutrient-intake (% of estimated need) cutoffs for mild/moderate/severe are in the full-text table and could not be quoted verbatim on access; recorded as GAPs, not asserted.
- GAP — WHO-vs-CDC growth-reference-per-age mapping NOT locatable verbatim in the accessed abstract; not asserted.
- Conflict (preserved, not collapsed) — vs NICE NG75 (src_20260718_rfgro002_09): NG75 expresses recognition triggers as UK-WHO centile spaces/centile lines; this AND/A.S.P.E.N. statement expresses severity as z-scores/percentiles on WHO/CDC references. The two convention systems are NOT interchangeable and must not be equated.
- Trust: this is an expert consensus (secondary synthesis), not an outcome-validated diagnostic rule; the accompanying literature notes ongoing need for validation of the criteria against long-term functional outcomes.
